Typed **Work → Expression → Manifestation** graph. Spans bind to a *manifestation_id + sha256*, never to a DOI (a DOI names a Work, and a Work has no bytes). Edges (`exact_copy_of`, `accepted_manuscript_of`, `predecessor_of`, `reports_same_study`, `supersedes`, `cites`) carry `status` (PROPOSED|ASSERTED) and a mandatory `basis`; the constructor **raises** on an edge with no basis, and on any attempt to ASSERT `exact_copy_of` from a title match. `attribution_targets()` widens an attribution only across an **ASSERTED span-preserving** edge; `predecessor_of` transfers nothing *even when ASSERTED* — peer review changes numbers. `MIN_WORDS=2500` is deleted: completeness is per-artifact-kind, and `judicial_opinion`/`statute_section`/`trial_registry_record` have `stub_floor=None` (complete at any length). Migration lost **0 of 67** texts.

**THE MEMO'S DIAGNOSIS POINTED AT THE WRONG ROWS.** The 6 rows `wp_fetch` tagged `working_paper` are mostly *not* working papers (Acemoglu & Restrepo 2019 is the real typeset JEP article; Goos 2014 is LSE's "Published version (Refereed)"). The actual contamination is **6 different rows, all labelled `publisher`, fetched before `wp_fetch` existed** — their bytes open with their own cover pages: Acemoglu & Restrepo (2020) JPE → "NBER WORKING PAPER SERIES"; Krueger (1993) QJE → "NBER WORKING PAPERS SERIES"; Goldin & Katz (1998) QJE → NBER via Harvard DASH; Bresnahan (2002) QJE → "This Draft: November 2000 / Forthcoming in the QJE"; Lin (2011) REStat → "WORKING PAPER NO. 09-17"; Gray (2013) → "EHES Working Paper Series".

## What I built

`/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/event_ledger.py` — Sol's item 8, implemented as specified.

**Components may only emit observations; labels exist only as reducer output.** Two things make that structural rather than advisory:

1. **Emit-time conclusion guard.** `Ledger.emit()` rejects any payload carrying a terminal conclusion — a key like `content_status`/`fulltext_source`, or a value like `"FULLTEXT"`/`"no free copy"`/`"high quality"`. Raw text quoted from the world (`header_text`, `identity_window`, `span`) is exempt: a fetched PDF may contain any word; a *component* may not conclude. `note` is deliberately **not** exempt — that is where a conclusion gets smuggled.
2. **Labels are never stored.** They are computed on read by seven pure reducers (`derive_backend_outcome`, `derive_route_status`, `derive_content_profile`, `derive_semantic_binding`, `derive_eligibility`, `derive_weight_components`, `derive_coverage_status`). `record_derivation` is bolted from the inside by a frame check, and downstream reducers never *trust* a recorded verdict — they re-derive.

All of Sol's distinctions hold: 429/503 → `BACKEND_FAILED` (never absence); 404 → `NOT_INDEXED_BY_THIS_BACKEND`; `route_complete` requires *every planned adapter to have a terminal outcome*; a budget stop → `SEARCH_FAILED`; `high_quality` cannot be expressed — `WeightComponents` has no scalar, only components with provenance. **15/15 invariants pass. `test_gate_is_wired.py` stays 16/16.**

## The replay — every false label, verified against real bytes

| shipped | the events earn |
|---|---|
| Autor 2015 (JEP) `FULLTEXT` | 535w of **aeaweb cookie banner** → `NOT_A_DOCUMENT` → INADMISSIBLE |
| Parry 2016 `FULLTEXT, 4,027w` | we hold **Yang-Hui He, "Mathematics: The Rise of the Machines"** (arXiv 2025, theorem-proving) → `DIFFERENT_WORK` |
| Autor/Levy/Murnane 2003: `"no free text"` | s2=404, openalex=429, s2/title=429, **arxiv=never returned** → route `INCOMPLETE` → `UNSEARCHED`, not absence |
| Acemoglu 2019 `working_paper` | false the *other* way — it **is** the JEP article → `SAME_WORK` → ADMISSIBLE |
| 4 rows' `fulltext_words` | describe text truncated away at 120k chars |

**The decisive finding (Case 6).** I tested `fulltext_source='working_paper'` — the label meant to catch Sol's P0 — against what the documents actually are. **Agreement: zero.** It marks 6 rows, *none* of which is a working paper. It misses all 4 that are — with unambiguous headers: `NBER WORKING PAPER SERIES / Working Paper 23285` (Acemoglu 2020, JPE), MIT Sloan `Paper 154` (Bresnahan 2002, QJE), `NBER Working Paper No. 3858` (Krueger 1993, QJE), Philadelphia Fed `WORKING PAPER NO. 09-17` (Lin 2011, REStat).

Quarantining on that label would have **deleted 3 genuine journal articles and left all 4 real working papers in the corpus** — the fabrication ships anyway. The label names the *script that ran*, not the document.

Corpus re-derived: of 27 rows called `FULLTEXT` — **17 ADMISSIBLE, 4 DISCOVERY_LEAD, 6 INADMISSIBLE**. 70/70 rows carry at least one label no event supports.

## Two things you should know

**The fabrication is armed, not fired.** `evidence_cards_v2.json` (21:24) *predates* the poisoned corpus (21:29) — zero fabricated cards have shipped. But all 6 rows are `usable_next_run=True`; `evidence_miner.py:1580` gates on `content_status != 'CITATION_ONLY'`, so the next mine run ingests the Yang-Hui He math paper as Parry et al. **One run away.**

**The guard caught me three times, and I did not weaken it.** My coverage reducer tried to read an `admissible` flag a component would have had to write (refactored to re-derive). My first binding reducer passed Parry as `SAME_WORK/ADMISSIBLE` — the exact defect, committed by the cure. My second *over*-corrected and called Autor 2003 "a stranger's paper" when its title page is undecodable cid glyphs — absence of evidence as evidence of absence, the same disease inverted. `DIFFERENT_WORK` now requires *positive* evidence; an unreadable header yields `UNRESOLVED`. I also briefly exempted `note` from the guard to make my own code pass, and reverted it.

**Not yet wired.** This is a standalone module plus replay; `wp_fetch.py` and `evidence_miner.py` still write their own labels. The next step is making `evidence_miner` gate on `derive_eligibility` instead of `content_status` — at which point the 4 working papers and the stranger's paper leave the evidence pool, and the visible judge scalar will likely fall.

## ALIGNMENT CENSUS

**Zero spans were aligned. Not one working paper became journal-attributable.** That is the correct answer and I did not manufacture one. But the census below is not the one the task expected, because **the memo's list of 6 is wrong in both directions** — and the real exposure is ~3× larger.

### 1. The load-bearing finding: peer review *did* change the numbers, and I can show it

For `10.1086/705716` (Acemoglu & Restrepo, JPE 2020) we hold **NBER WP 23285 (March 2017)**. Comparing the working paper we mined against the **publisher's own published abstract** (OpenAlex, free metadata):

| | employment-to-pop | wages |
|---|---|---|
| **NBER WP 23285** (what we hold + mined) | **0.37 pp** | **0.73%** |
| **Published JPE 2020** (article of record) | **0.2 pp** | **0.42%** |

The headline estimate was cut by nearly half in peer review. And evidence card #1 currently reads `effect: "0.37 percentage points lower"`, `attribution: "Writing in the Journal of Political Economy in 2020, Acemoglu and Restrepo"`. **The artifact was one compose-run from printing 0.37 and crediting it to a journal that says 0.2.** Sol's risk is not theoretical on this corpus; it is realised, on the single most-quoted number in it.

Note the failure shape: the span *is* verbatim. The gate would have passed it. The document named is simply not the document the span came from.

So of the 6: **one** is a working paper, **three** are already-admissible journal articles being needlessly withheld, one is a cookie banner, one is a foreign work. The memo's "1,691 quantitative claims" is also wrong — these 6 rows hold **649** numbers, and **487 of them are in idx 9, the admissible AER article**.

### 4. Bottom line

**119 of 192 evidence cards (62%) are mined from documents that are not the journal article they are attributed to.** Only **70** sit on admissible bytes. Task 72 demands journal articles only, so all 119 must be withheld — the weaker corpus wins, exactly as Sol said. The three genuinely-admissible economics papers (JEP 2019, AER 2014, ET&P) currently contribute **zero** cards, and Autor (2015) is now re-minable from the real JEP article: that is where the recovery lies, not in the working papers.

Two further defects surfaced: **65 of 192 card spans no longer verify against the current corpus bytes at all** (cards mined 21:24, corpus overwritten 21:29 — the bytes churned underneath them), and **idx 66 has a card mined from bytes that are not readable prose** (stopword density 0.0126).

### 5. My own harness failed its first audit — reporting that too

My first pass reported three papers "ALIGNED — all spans verified in the publishedVersion." **They were not aligned.** Unpaywall's `publishedVersion` URL pointed at the same Springer PDF already in the corpus, and I had verified the text against itself (8-gram coverage 1.000 — same document). A circularity guard now requires the journal bytes to be a *distinct document* before any span counts. Same failure shape as everything else tonight: a label asserting more than its content supported.

Two labels I refused to soften: AEA and SAGE returned **HTTP 403** (a bot block). That is recorded as `ACCESS_BLOCKED` — a fact about our request — never as "no free copy." I did not spoof a browser UA to evade it.

One correction to the tooling: **Unpaywall labels the LSE deposit of Goos-Manning-Salomons `submittedVersion`, and it is wrong.** The bytes carry `American Economic Review 2014, 104(8): 2509–2526`, alternating publisher running heads, and printed page folios — furniture only a typesetter emits. The bytes overruled the metadata.

# ADVERSARIAL REPORT — 6 of 6 ATTACKS SUCCEED

## First, the mandated check: `test_gate_is_wired.py` is **16/16 PASS**, and **nothing was weakened**

```
** GATE IS WIRED AND ON THE CRITICAL PATH. **
EXIT=0
```
- `git diff HEAD --stat` on `test_gate_is_wired.py`, `cellcog_composer.py`, `synthesis_contract.py`, `evidence_miner.py` → **empty**. Test file sha1 `e14ff7d1…` == `HEAD:scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py`. 18 `check()` calls, 16 executed.
- I modified **zero** tracked files. I added only 6 attack harnesses (`/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/adv_attack*.py`).

**And that is the finding.** The canary is green, `provenance.py --self-test` is **18/18 ALL PASS**, and five of six attacks still succeed. Nothing was weakened — *the checks certify a lane the fabrication no longer uses.*

## The root cause, one line

**`scripts/provenance.py` is a closed loop.** The module built this round to enforce Sol's rule is called by nobody:

```
journal_attributable   called outside provenance.py by: NOTHING
attribution_targets    called outside provenance.py by: NOTHING
bind_span              called outside provenance.py by: NOTHING
```
`event_ledger.py` is imported by **nothing at all**. `version_align.py`/`alignment_census.py` import only `profile()` — the profiler, never the rule. **This is the exact bug from `test_gate_is_wired.py`'s own docstring — `validate()` imported and never called — reproduced in the fix for it.** `BACKEND_FAILED` exists *only* inside those two uncalled scripts.

---

## 1. 429 RENDERED AS "NO COPY" — **ATTACK SUCCEEDS**
Forced total HTTP 429 on Autor/Levy/Murnane, whose free copy provably exists (NBER WP 8337). Real `deep_fetch.main()` and `wp_fetch.main()`:
```
  [ 1/1] still paywalled  4743 cites | Autor et al. (2003), QJE      <- deep_fetch
  [ 1/1] no free text        Autor (2003)                            <- wp_fetch
  content_status now : CITATION_ONLY
  any BACKEND_FAILED marker on the row? NONE
```
A fact about our request rate, written to disk as a fact about the world. `CITATION_ONLY` is the miner's *exclusion* label (`evidence_miner.py:1580`) — a throttle silently and permanently deletes a paper from the evidence base. `deep_fetch.py:137` `mark = 'RECOVERED' if … else 'still paywalled'`.

## 2. ABSTRACT RENDERED AS FULL TEXT — **ATTACK SUCCEEDS**
`deep_fetch.py:105,116` still gates at **500 words** and writes `content_status='FULLTEXT'` at `:130`.
```
  >>> deep_fetch labelled a 654-word ABSTRACT as: FULLTEXT
```
`corpus_truth.py` and `provenance.profile()` *do* repel it — **but the fix stopped at the label and never reached the admission decision.** Frey & Osborne is `ABSTRACT_ONLY`, its bytes are the **Oxford ORA web page** (`'…- ORA - Oxford University Research Archive CONTACT Name Ema…'`, 548 words, `artifact_kind=landing_page`, `complete=False`), the miner admits it (`content_status != CITATION_ONLY`), and **4 live cards cite it as "Frey and Osborne (2017), Technological Forecasting and Social Change"** — spans verified present in the landing page. This is the paper the synthesis leaned on.

## 3. PREDECESSOR RENDERED AS JOURNAL VERSION — **ATTACK SUCCEEDS (THE P0)**
The composer's faithfulness gate — the one the canary certifies — **admitted this, with zero reasons**:
> `'Writing in the Journal of Political Economy in 2020, Acemoglu et al. report that we analyze the effect of the increase in industrial robot usage between 1990 and 2007 on US local labor markets.'`
> `>>> reasons: []`

The span is verbatim in bytes whose header reads **`NBER WORKING PAPER SERIES`**. **Six rows carry a journal attribution over working-paper bytes** — Acemoglu (2020) JPE, Bresnahan (2002) QJE, Krueger (1993) QJE, Goldin (1998) QJE, Lin (2011) REStat, Gray (2013). `evidence_miner.py:1195` copies `attribution` straight off the corpus row; the card carries **no `manifestation_id`, no `content_hash`** — it cannot say which bytes it came from. **8 cards on disk already cite Bresnahan's working paper as The Quarterly Journal of Economics.** The gate checks the span against the bytes we hold; it never asks *which document those bytes are*, and the module that can answer is called by nobody.

## 4. DUPLICATE REPORTS AS INDEPENDENT STUDIES — **ATTACK SUCCEEDS**
```
[CLOSED] Cross-cutting x Job displacement  n_works=2  dois=['10.3386/w23285', '10.1086/705716']
     reason: 2 works, 2 quantitative + 0 qualitative result(s)
```
One study — Acemoglu–Restrepo "Robots and Jobs" — reported twice (NBER WP: **0.37pp**; JPE: **0.2pp**) **closed a coverage cell as two independent works.** `Cell.n_works = len(self.dois)` (`research_contract.py:718`), `MIN_WORKS_PER_CELL = 2`. Nothing asks whether two DOIs report the same study; the 52 `reports_same_study` edges in the provenance graph are never consulted. Worse than double-counting: it double-counts a number *that peer review changed*, and `consolidate()` returns `n_sources=[1,1]` — they don't even merge, so both stand as citable findings that disagree.

## 5. LEXICAL MISS RENDERED AS A GAP — **ATTACK SUCCEEDS**
Three cards, each a measured wage finding, each containing the word *wages*:
```
  c1: 'We find that wages fell by 4.1 percent following the adoption of industrial robots.'
  routed anywhere?   NO CELL CLAIMED ANY OF THEM
  UNROUTED           : ['c1', 'c2', 'c3']
  [GAP] Manufacturing x Wages  n_works=0 :: 0 groundable work(s) in the corpus (need 2)
```
**The dimension named `Wages` is structurally incapable of matching the word "wage".** `build_matchers` (`research_contract.py:618`) drops a stem shared by ≥2 terms in the family; "wage" appears in *Skill premium* ("wage inequality", "wage dispersion"), so it is disqualified. Wages routes only on `hourly wage`/`compensation`/`earning`/`hourly`/`income`/`salary`. The artifact prints **"What the reviewed literature does not cover"** (`:994`); `unrouted` is emitted only by `print_matrix()`, a **console diagnostic** (`:1249`). There is no `UNROUTED` or `SEARCH_FAILED` state in the artifact — **a matcher miss and a genuine absence of evidence produce the identical output.**

## 6. A LEGAL SOURCE REJECTED FOR LACKING NUMBERS — **ATTACK SUCCEEDS**
Real miner, **real LLM**, real judicial opinion (a Title VII holding on algorithmic hiring):
```
  LLM calls          : 2
  candidates harvested: 0
  LLM proposed       : 0
  CARDS PRODUCED     : 0
  rejects            : []          <-- THE LOSS IS NOT EVEN COUNTED
```
`harvest()` (`evidence_miner.py:918`): `if not re.search(r'\d', t): continue` — **a sentence with no digit is never a candidate**, and the discard is not counted in `rejects` or `stats`. `MINE_PROMPT:1254` opens *"mining a peer-reviewed article for INTERPRETABLE QUANTITATIVE EVIDENCE"* and orders *"If you cannot fill effect + unit + outcome, DO NOT EMIT THE OBJECT."* A source of the first rank vanished leaving **no trace in the pipeline's own self-report of what it discarded.**

**Honest nuance:** `gate_card()` does *not* strictly demand a number — I fed it the holding with `outcome` filled and it was **ADMITTED**. The demand for a *measurement* lives at recall, at the prompt, and at cell-close (`has_direct_result` requires a result verb; the holding has none → `n_qual=0` → *"the corpus mentions this cell but does not measure it"*). The gate is innocent; the three stages around it are not.

## The shape, again
`source_version` on every card is `sha256(text)[:12]` — a *tamper hash*, named as if it were a *version identity*. Even the field name asserts more than its content supports.
